Facing a poor IPL season, Yadav's form is under scrutiny, with his ambition of leading India to the 2028 Olympics potentially at risk. Coach Mahela Jayawardene defended his player, highlighting his talent and past brilliance.

title: From Ranchi school where Dhoni studied, 7-year-old swimming prodigy makes history
No non-veg, no alcohol: After Iftar row, how Varanasi boatmen are changing rules on the Ganga
How Narmada cruise went from leisure outing to tragedy
Ashoka Road accident: Killer truck got special permission from Delhi traffic cops despite 5 challans
description: A truck, granted special permission for waste collection despite multiple challans, fatally struck two cousins on Ashoka Road. The permit's validity until 2026 is now under review for cancellation. Police are conducting raids in Bihar to apprehend the driver, while the victims' family expresses distress over the lack of updates and arrests.

Jamieson’s fiery send-off to Sooryavanshi sparks outrage, debate erupts
description: A heated moment between Kyle Jamieson and young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi marked an IPL 2026 match. Jamieson's aggressive send-off after dismissing the 15-year-old drew reactions. Rajasthan Royals posted 225, led by Riyan Parag's 90. Delhi Capitals chased it down with KL Rahul and Pathum Nissanka starring. This win was Delhi's highest IPL chase.

'Will strengthen presence in Andaman Sea ': After Rahul’s attack, govt defends Great Nicobar project
description: The government has clarified the Rs 81,000 crore Great Nicobar Project, emphasizing its strategic importance for India's presence in the Andaman Sea and Southeast Asia. Officials stated that environmental safeguards and the protection of indigenous communities are core to the planning, with all statutory procedures and consultations meticulously followed to ensure no adverse impact on Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups.

title: 'Chief concern ': India continues to be on US priority watch list on intellectual property rights
description: India remains on the US Trade Representative's priority watch list for intellectual property concerns, citing inconsistent progress and long-standing issues with its Patents Act. The report highlights worries over data protection for pharma and agri-chemical products, alongside high customs duties on IP-intensive goods. Weak enforcement of copyrights and trademarks also remain key issues.

title: How job loss, debt pushed Indian youth to suicide in US
description: A 26-year-old Indian student in Chicago, Chandu Iraganaboyina, tragically took his own life due to joblessness and severe financial stress. Friends and community members are now working to repatriate his body to India, highlighting the immense pressure faced by some international students. Efforts are underway to understand how he obtained a firearm.

title: Can’t lose sight of Himanta’s unparliamentary remarks: SC
description: The Supreme Court granted anticipatory bail to Congress spokesperson Pawan Khera, deeming allegations against Assam CM's wife and her counter-allegations politically motivated. The court emphasized personal liberty, stating custodial interrogation wasn't warranted given the political overtones of the dispute.

title: UP Congress chief Ajay Rai hospitalised after fainting at event in Lucknow
description: UP Congress chief Ajay Rai was hospitalized after fainting during a Lucknow event. Initially feared to be a stroke or seizure, doctors now suspect sodium depletion caused his collapse. While his blood pressure was high, preliminary tests show no serious brain injury. Rai has regained consciousness and is stable, easing concerns among party members.

title: Gujarat businessman in US fined Rs 15 crore for illegal 16-year stay
description: A Gujarati businessman residing illegally in the US for 16 years faces a USD 1.8 million civil penalty for defying a removal order. The fine, calculated at over USD 998 daily, was imposed despite his pending U visa application. This significant penalty highlights a rare instance involving a Gujarati national, with thousands of similar notices issued to other migrants.

title: Bangladesh Foreign Ministry lodges formal protest over Assam CM’s comments
Crew ‘abandoned’ cruise before it sank; no life jackets given, claims Bargi Dam survivor
description: A Bargi Dam cruise survivor alleges pilot and crew abandoned passengers before the boat sank, leaving them to fend for themselves. Despite a sudden storm, lifejackets were reportedly unavailable initially. Nine have died, six are missing, and a high-level probe has been ordered. Officials are struggling to confirm passenger numbers due to unregistered boardings.

title: Wedding drama: Bride garlands lover instead of groom, runs off stage; guests left stunned
description: A wedding in Chhindwara took a dramatic turn when the bride garlanded her lover instead of the groom, leading to chaos and a scuffle. The groom's side left without completing the ceremony, with the bride's family reportedly assaulting the man and forcing him out.

ACP-rank officer held for flashing 9-year-old girl in Mumbai
description: A Deputy Superintendent of Police rank officer was arrested in Mumbai for allegedly flashing a nine-year-old girl in a Worli garden. The accused, attached to the DG office's Wireless department, was apprehended after a complaint and sent to judicial custody. The incident, which occurred on April 23, is being investigated, with the minor's statement recorded.

description: West Bengal's political landscape, historically dominated by single parties for extended periods, is now witnessing an intense battle for the 2026 assembly elections. Chief minister Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule faces a formidable challenge from the aggressively expanding BJP, which aims to end the TMC's reign. This election marks a critical juncture for the state's political legacy.

Jobs are up, but so is the gap: Decoding India’s wage reality
description: While India's workforce participation has increased, a deeper look reveals significant disparities. More people are working, but many are in self-employment or informal roles with uncertain incomes. Gender and location heavily influence access to stable, well-paying jobs, highlighting a critical imbalance in job quality and opportunity.

title: CEO says ‘When Google CEO agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
description: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.
